title: 2026 Golden Globes Predictions: Entertainment Editor's Picks
text: Philip Mutz, VP of News and Entertainment at PureWow, shares his predictions for the 83rd annual Golden Globes ceremony airing January 11, 2026 on CBS and Paramount+, hosted by Nikki Glaser. For Best Motion Picture Drama, Mutz predicts Sinners, calling it his favorite 2025 film describing it as scary, sexy, thrilling, hilarious and littered with meaning. For Best Actress in Drama, Jessie Buckley for Hamnet is described as practically a shoo-in, with Mutz citing her weathered, freckled face capable of both raw pain and hardened perseverance. For Best Motion Picture Comedy/Musical, One Battle After Another wins this prediction, noting its recent Critics' Choice Award victories for Best Picture, Director, and Adapted Screenplay. For Best Actor Comedy/Musical, Timothée Chalamet for Marty Supreme is predicted, following momentum from his Critics' Choice win. Best Actress Comedy/Musical prediction goes to Amanda Seyfried for The Testament of Ann Lee, portraying the Shakers religious movement founder. In television, Adolescence is predicted for Best Limited Series following its multiple Emmy wins. The Pitt is predicted for Best Drama Series timing its win with season two's HBO debut. Noah Wyle for The Pitt is predicted as Best Drama Actor, who also won the Emmy. Rhea Seehorn for Pluribus is praised for her compelling solo scenes for Best Drama Actress. The Studio is predicted for Best Comedy/Musical Series featuring Seth Rogen, Bryan Cranston, and Catherine O'Hara.
